---
title: "It doesn't say anywhere it has to be good"
date: 2026-03-02
categories: ['thoughts']
---

I think we borrow too much from religious—namely Abrahamic—narratives that 'there is a greater plan'. Therefore we seem to think that things can turn out well even if we don't try.

I disagree.

The loving husband/father is that way because they try and put in the work. The default father/husband might be an asshole. 
In much the same way AI's impact on the world needn't be good. Heck, [most futures might be bad](https://nickbostrom.com/papers/vulnerable.pdf).

We need to put in the work, the effort and make the sacrifice to ensure a positive future.
